rules: You are improving the CoreLifecycle video pipeline. HARD RULES (never violate, even though permissions are off): (1) NEVER publish, unlist, upload, or touch any live video or the YouTube API. (2) NEVER read, write, move, or commit anything under secrets/. (3) NEVER edit scripts/yt_upload.py or any publish path, and NEVER write runs/last_post.txt or ops/produced_topics.json. (4) One story per iteration. (5) VERIFY GATE before marking a story passed: run `python3 build.py` and it MUST exit 0 (quality gate + 1-frame smoke render); also run `git diff --name-only` and confirm it touches nothing under secrets/ and no publish path. (6) If build.py fails OR the diff is out of bounds: run `git checkout -- .` to DISCARD the code change, write the blocker into the story's notes and progress.txt, do NOT set passes:true, do NOT commit the code change, and stop. (7) Keep progress.txt notes TERSE (caveman-brief), facts only. (8) `build.py` rewrites src/episode_meta.json, src/timeline.json, and out/ as a side effect — before committing your passing story, run `git checkout -- src/episode_meta.json src/timeline.json` so your commit contains ONLY your improvement, not stale last-render churn.

userStories.title: package before review
userStories.notes: mob_boss (07-01) got a 'revise' verdict whose ONLY blocker was pipeline order: daily_autopilot renders out/thumbnail.png + gen_packaging.py (upload_kit.json) at step 5 AFTER the review loop, so the reviewer sees the PREVIOUS episode's stale files on disk and correctly refuses to approve (a Roman 'YOU ARE MORTAL' thumb would have shipped on the Mafia video if not caught). Cheap fix: move `npx remotion still Thumbnail` + `python3 gen_packaging.py` to right after build (or into build.py step order) so the on-disk packaging always matches the episode under review; publish step can re-run them idempotently. Costs one 5s still per build; saves a whole render->review->fix cycle. MITIGATED MANUALLY on billionaire_heir (07-03) AND mexican_cartel (07-06): showrunner rendered the Thumbnail still (out/thumbnail.png) + ran gen_packaging.py (out/upload_kit.json) right after BUILD OK, so on-disk packaging matches the episode under review. On mexican_cartel this also CAUGHT the number-thumb collision [[number-thumb-collision-short-values]] before review — rendering the thumb post-build is doubling as a thumbnail QA gate, another reason to make it a permanent pipeline step. The pipeline-order fix in daily_autopilot/build.py is still the real cure.

userStories.title: template must be registry key not backdrop
userStories.notes: could_you_survive_ocean (07-10) was resumed from an INTERRUPTED run whose content.py referenced TWO scene backdrops as if they were templates: t01 template='seaDeck' (seaDeck is a DYNASTY *backdrop*; the template that renders it is yachtDeck) and t13 template='stormSea' (stormSea is a *backdrop* used by oceanCapsize, not a template). The gate correctly HALTed ('template X not in registry') but only AFTER voice+music+bundle ran (~minutes). This is a recurring interrupted-run hazard because a backdrop and a template often share a concept name (seaDeck/yachtDeck, stormSea/oceanCapsize). Fixes shipped this run: added two proper OCEAN templates — boatDeck (working-sailor comfort beat on the seaDeck backdrop, distinct from DYNASTY yachtDeck's smug heir) and rainSquall (the squall/rain-catch mercy beat on the stormSea backdrop). CHEAP GUARD IDEA: build.py already imports content + can import PACK_TEMPLATES keys via a tiny node/grep — assert every scene['template'] is a PACK_TEMPLATES key BEFORE the expensive voice step (move the registry check that gate.py already does to the top, next to the syntax gate), so a bad template name fails in seconds, not minutes. Pairs with [[meta-topic-mismatch-guard]] (both catch interrupted/partial-run state cheaply).

userStories.title: scaleterror caption pill collision
userStories.notes: could_you_survive_ocean (07-10): the SCALETERROR archetype (thumbs.tsx ThumbScaleTerror) renders the KICKER+L1 caption as a Punch at (70,150) fs48 and the red KeyPill at (78, 150+kw) where kw~=88 for a 9-char keyword — the pill's rotated box top reaches up into the caption's baseline, so 'NO RESCUE' sat ON TOP of '1,200 MILES FROM LAND' and hid all but the trailing 'AND' (looked broken). Also for an OCEAN topic the giant INK humanoid 'threat' shape reads as a looming PERSON, not the sea — a confusing metaphor for a water-survival video. Rendering the thumbnail post-build (the [[package-before-review]] habit) caught both before review; switched to the eyeline archetype (big worried face + arrow + withheld '?' + NO RESCUE) which rendered clean and on-emotion. CHEAP FIXES for scaleterror: (a) push the KICKER+L1 caption ABOVE the pill (y small, e.g. y=60) or move the pill down so its box never overlaps the caption; (b) for scale-terror on NON-humanoid threats (ocean, avalanche, fire) let a meta field pick the looming shape (wave/wall vs figure). Pairs with [[number-thumb-collision-short-values]] + [[beforeafter-hero-expr-hardcoded]] — same family of thumbnail layout gaps.

userStories.title: scenario money spine validated
userStories.notes: trillionaire (07-07) validated the SCENARIO format (first non-rank/non-history/non-survival episode) AND a fourth spine flavor: a PURE MONEY ladder where the on-screen gold number CLIMBS ($0 -> $1M -> $100M -> $1B -> $100B -> $1T) while the SUB-caption tracks what you can actually SPEND FALLING to nothing ('NOT CASH · A PROMISE ON PAPER' -> 'YOU NEVER SELL' -> 'YOU CAN'T SELL IT · CASH OUT AND THE NUMBER DIES'). The drama is the GAP between the exploding paper number and the collapsing spendable amount — the apex twist is that a trillionaire owns, spendably, $0. Distinct from [[valuation-vs-personal-spine]] (built vs kept), [[inverted-spine-validated]] (power vs frozen pay), [[control-vs-own-spine-validated]] (control vs own): here it's PAPER vs SPENDABLE. Composed entirely from STARTUP (garageStart/startupGrow/serverScale/ipoBell) + FINANCE (tradingFloor/pnlWall) + DYNASTY (galaBallroom/yachtDeck/familyVault) + generic (podiumScene) + universal — NO bespoke TSX, 28 scenes, 13.0 min, gate PASS 0 warnings. Reusable for lottery_winner, and any scenario where escalating MILLIONS/DECISIONS are the level labels. The two share-beats (trillion seconds = ~31,700 yrs; $1M/day since Christ ~= $740B, still not $1T) are arithmetically verified and landed as the 'wait, that's real?' plants (research doc trillionaire.md).

userStories.title: worn swords hip prop
userStories.notes: samurai (07-04) made THE WEIGHT OF THE TWO SWORDS AT THE LEFT HIP the sensory anchor motif — it 'arrives' at t04 (daisho), is re-triggered at every level-up ('the swords are heavier today'), and is stripped bare at t25 (Haitōrei ban, 'your hip goes light'). But the daishō is only DRAWN once, on the sword stand at t04; the POV figure never visibly WEARS the swords across the climb, and the t25 payoff ('hip goes bare') is audio-only. Same failure family as [[want-object-visual-plant]] (mob_boss's undrawn Cadillac). Fix: a small 'sheathed daishō at the left hip' figure primitive in stage.tsx (a short + long line at the belt) that the SAMURAI templates toggle ON from L2 and OFF at the ban scene — so the anchor the narration keeps tightening is actually visible tightening, and the empty hip at the end reads at a glance. Generalizes to any worn want-object anchor (a ring, a badge, a holster).

userStories.title: control vs own spine validated
userStories.notes: real_estate_mogul ep validated a LEVERAGE spine: the on-screen big number is ASSETS YOU CONTROL ($0 -> 300K -> 4M -> 40M -> 250M -> 2B -> 600B -> $393T) driven by debt + other people's money, while the personal cut / down payment / equity lags in the gold SUB-caption ('~$60K DOWN', 'NONE OF IT YOURS'). It's the third spine flavor after [[valuation-vs-personal-spine]] (what you BUILT vs KEEP) and [[inverted-spine-validated]] (power vs frozen pay): here it's what you CONTROL vs what you OWN. Reusable for any leverage/OPM ladder (private equity, leveraged trading, franchising). Apex flips to the asset class itself + the capital that prices it.

userStories.title: smoke covers only endpoints
userStories.notes: build.py smoke render only checks frame 0 (which is the fade-in -> BLANK) + the last frame, so a bespoke prop/backdrop used only mid-scene is NEVER smoke-tested and a render-time error or an ugly composition (the roman 'chariot' first read as ambiguous blobs) ships unseen. For roman I had to hand-render ~12 mid-scene stills to catch it. Cheap fix: when content.py introduces NEW template keys not in the previous episode, build.py should auto-render one mid-scene still per new template (pick frame = scene.startFrame + dur/2 from timeline.json) into out/_smoke_packs/ and HALT/warn on render error. Closes the QA gap between the 1-frame smoke and the post-render reviewer.

userStories.title: voice gen cache and parallel
userStories.notes: Follow-up to [[edge-tts-retry-resilience]]. gen_voice_edge.py still re-synthesizes ALL 28 scenes from the network on every build run, even when only content changed for a few (or a prior run already produced 27 good wavs). Two cheap wins: (1) per-scene CACHE keyed by a hash of (narration text + VOICE + RATE) -> skip synth+master when the cached wav matches, so a HALT-and-rerun reuses good scenes and only the failed/edited one re-hits the network (turns a ~2-3min voice step into seconds on reruns); (2) optionally a small bounded asyncio.gather pool (2-3 concurrent) to shorten cold builds. Caution: cache must invalidate on any narration edit (hash the text, not just the scene id) or it ships stale audio. Lives in gen_voice_edge.py.

userStories.title: number thumb collision short values
userStories.notes: mexican_cartel (07-06): the NUMBER archetype (thumbs.tsx ThumbNumber) collides for SHORT $ values. BIG font = min(480, 1300/len*1.6), so a 3-char value like '$85' maxes at 480px and the giant number sits over the top kicker/line1 caption — 'A HIT PAYS' was almost entirely hidden behind the '$85', only '..YS' peeked out (looked broken). Had to abandon the strongest hook (a life costs $85 = DISPOSABLE) and switch the thumb to beforeafter (NOBODY -> HUNTED). Cheap fix: when BIG.length is small, cap fs lower (e.g. min(360,...)) AND/OR move the kicker+line1 caption OUT of the number's box (top-left small, or below the accent bar) so a short shock-number thumb is usable. The low-horror-number thumb ($85/$30-a-hit) is a high-CTR angle worth unlocking. Pairs with [[negative-overlay-recurring]] (both are thumbnail/overlay number-treatment gaps).
acceptanceCriteria: The improvement described in notes is implemented: mexican_cartel (07-06): the NUMBER archetype (thumbs.tsx ThumbNumber) collides for SHORT $ values. userStories.title: sound anchor sidesteps undrawn prop
userStories.notes: trillionaire used a SOUND/EMOTIONAL anchor — Dani's 19-SECOND VOICEMAIL (her laugh, 'eat something, call me back'): saved t02, replayed t16, she calls LIVE at the trillion and you let it ring t18 (cold-open payoff), replayed one last time at loop-close t25. Because the anchor is inherently a SOUND, not a worn/carried object, it sidesteps the recurring 'audio-only anchor never drawn on the figure' defect family ([[worn-swords-hip-prop]], [[dog-tags-anchor-visual-plant]], [[want-object-visual-plant]]) — there is nothing to draw, so nothing is missing visually; the '19 SECONDS' overlay at t25 carries the payoff on-screen. LESSON: when a topic has no natural WORN object, a recurring sound/recording/number anchor tied to a named person is a clean choice that avoids the undrawn-prop trap entirely and still gives the overlay a concrete callback ('19 SECONDS'). Secondary sensory texture (burnt $4.50 gas-station coffee) rode alongside as the 'money never upgrades this about you' motif.

userStories.title: dog tags anchor visual plant
userStories.notes: special_forces made 'dog tags, TAPED so they don't rattle' (sound discipline) the sensory anchor — loud at selection (t01), taped before the first infil (t05), checked on the team (t07), NONE/sterile at the Unit (t10), a dead man's tags carried out at the midpoint (t14), a drawer of them (t17), a new candidate's rattling at the loop-close (t28). Strong callback engine, but like samurai's daishō [[worn-swords-hip-prop]] and the mob Cadillac [[want-object-visual-plant]] it is AUDIO-ONLY — the taped tags are never drawn on the POV figure, so the 'sterile/empty' and 'drawer of the dead' payoffs don't read visually. Same fix family: a tiny 'tags at the chest' figure primitive that MILITARY/SPY templates toggle ON (loose) at L1, TAPED from L2, OFF (sterile) at L4, so the anchor the narration keeps tightening tightens on screen. Generalizes to any small worn/carried anchor object (ring, badge, tags, coin).

userStories.title: fix agent artifact hygiene
userStories.notes: docs/research/mob_boss.md ended with stray literal '</content>\n</invoke>' lines — a prior agent's tool-call syntax leaked into the written file. Harmless here (research doc), but if it happened inside content.py or episode_meta.json the syntax gate would HALT a build. Idea: build.py (or gate.py) grep the creative-owned text files for tool-call markers (</invoke>, </content>, <function_results) and warn, so a corrupted write is caught at build time, not at review.

userStories.title: inverted spine validated
userStories.notes: soldier ep proved an INVERTED spine: the on-screen big number is NON-MONEY power (TROOPS UNDER YOUR COMMAND: 0 -> 9 -> 150 -> 700 -> 4,500 -> 20,000 -> 200,000) while PERSONAL PAY stays small, flat, and CAPS in the sub-caption (~$25K -> ~$221K). The drama is the GAP between exploding power and frozen pay — the opposite of the finance ladders where money is the spine. Apex flips to the system that POINTS the force ($895B budget / $2.7T global spend) and the civilians who never bleed. Reusable for politician (constituents/votes commanded vs a fixed salary), and any rank/authority ladder where power, not pay, is the escalation. RE-VALIDATED by spy: REACH (ASSETS 0 -> 4 -> ~20 -> ONE STATION -> A REGION -> EVERY COVERT OP -> 21,000 OFFICERS -> THE PEOPLE ABOVE) explodes in the overlay while GS pay stays capped in the sub ($66K -> ~$200K, 'you never get rich'); the only $ count-up is the apex $106B secret budget at cold-open + close. Pairs with [[non-finance-spine-validated]].
acceptanceCriteria: The improvement described in notes is implemented: soldier ep proved an INVERTED spine: the on-screen big number is NON-MONEY power (TROOPS UNDER YOUR COMMAND: 0 -> 9 -> 150 -> 700 -> 4,500 -> 20,000 -> 200,000) while PERSONAL PAY stays small, flat, and CAPS in the sub-caption (~$25K -> ~$221K). userStories.title: restage reused template by level
userStories.notes: spy reviewer (approve, score 8) flagged that the 'station' template at LEVEL 04 (chief of station, t11) is visually near-identical to LEVEL 02 (case officer, t04) — both correct & non-adjacent, so no BIBLE rule broken, but the jump in REACH reads only from the overlay text, not the staging. When the same topic-appropriate template is the right fit at two different LEVELS, the director engine could scale the staging by level depth (wider frame / more background figures / bigger desk count at the higher rung) so a reused template still reads as escalation, not repetition. Generalizes beyond spy (any ladder where one setting is the natural home for two ranks: tradingFloor analyst->PM, operatingRoom resident->attending). Cheap win in src/stage.tsx or director.tsx; pairs with the multi-shot director. RECONFIRMED by mob_boss (07-01): reviewer flagged the 'two figures at a bar' look recurring ~6x across redSauce+socialClub (associate rules -> made-man earn -> capo sit-down -> Sal's murder -> the rat) with no visual escalation between ranks; one swap (t18->backAlley) reduced it but the by-level staging scaler remains the real fix.

userStories.title: faceless secondary figures
userStories.notes: spy reviewer noted secondary characters (Yusuf at dinner, junior officers in debriefs, boardroom figures) render as grey faceless silhouettes against the always-faced black POV figure. In spy it read as a deliberate 'they stopped being faces' motif and was consistent, so harmless here — but confirm it's intentional per-topic: for warmer relationship beats (a mentor, a spouse, a co-founder) a faced secondary figure could land more empathy. Low priority; flag only because it could read as a defect on a topic where the other person is meant to be sympathetic.

userStories.title: centered landmark figure collision
userStories.notes: heirGates first render placed the POV figure at x=960 — dead center on the estateGrounds fountain (also centered) — so the basin+jet read as a thought bubble sitting on the figure's head. Fixed by offsetting the figure to x=700 (still behind the bars, clear of fountain + crest). Rule of thumb for new templates: when a backdrop has a centered landmark (fountain, crest, vault door, podium), stage the figure off-center; the mid-scene pack still (per [[smoke-covers-only-endpoints]]) is what caught it.

userStories.title: want object visual plant
userStories.notes: mob_boss: the black Cadillac (the named want-object opening and closing the loop) was never drawn — the cyclical echo was audio-only. Rule of thumb: if the script names a physical want-object in scene 1 and the loop close, stage.tsx should actually draw it in both (a small prop primitive is enough); the visual rhyme is what makes the ending feel circular.

userStories.title: beforeafter hero expr hardcoded
userStories.notes: mexican_cartel (07-06): ThumbBefore (beforeafter archetype) hardcodes the AFTER hero's face to expr='smug' (`expr={face('smug')}`) and ignores meta thumb.expr, so a 'HUNTED'/fear pairing renders a calm neutral stare instead of the worried/shock face that is the #1 CTR lever with no real faces. ThumbPov already reads t.expr correctly. One-line fix: make ThumbBefore's Hero use expr={face(t.expr || 'smug')} so the after-figure's emotion can match the after-word (shock/worried for a HUNTED/BURNED/TRAPPED apex). Rendered fine this run (cold narco reads OK), flagged as a cheap CTR upgrade.

userStories.title: runtime insensitive to word cuts
userStories.notes: mexican_cartel (07-06): cut ~85 words (2725->2643) and runtime only moved 13.8->13.5 min, because per-scene GAP padding (0.25 default + LEAD) plus the deliberate gap=0.7/1.4 dramatic holds dominate a 29-scene episode, not the narration length. LESSON for over-length episodes: the effective runtime levers are (a) drop a whole scene (~27s each) or (b) reduce/relocate a dramatic gap — shaving words gives diminishing returns (~0.2s per word). Note: 13.5 min still shipped (founder 14.1 min is the top performer, roman 13.1 min shipped), so the ~13 ceiling is soft with cloud render; but if a HALT ever needs real trimming, cut scenes, not words. Could add a build.py readout of 'padding vs speech' seconds so the writer sees where the runtime actually goes.
